meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no SaaS knowledge (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no SaaS knowledge. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for AI Builders: A Student's Guide
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no SaaS knowledge' barrier. Learn to implement analytics in your first app using Antigravity and Claude Code with this beginner-friendly guide.
content.answer_block: To bridge your SaaS knowledge gap, implement lightweight analytics immediately. By integrating event tracking into your first project, you transform guesswork into data-driven decisions. Use Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code to automate the instrumentation of your user journey, providing clear visibility into how real users interact with your app.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks agonizing over code, pushing commits, and dreaming of your first launch. But when you finally deploy, you’re greeted by a deafening silence. Without a feedback loop, you are essentially flying blind, unable to distinguish between a UI bug and a lack of market interest. 

This isn't a failure of your coding ability; it is a failure of your infrastructure. By integrating analytics from day one, you transition from a coder to a product engineer. This guide provides the blueprint to instrument your application, turning raw traffic into actionable intelligence so you can iterate with surgical precision.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the one action that proves your app delivers value. For a student project, this is usually 'Account Created' or 'First Query Run'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Product Manager. Analyze my app's core loop and define 3 key events I should track to validate user engagement for a portfolio project.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Identify the core user conversion path in this repository and propose tracking events for each step.'

steps.title: Initialize Analytics Provider
steps.description: We will use a developer-friendly analytics SDK that allows for easy event logging without heavy configuration overhead.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a boilerplate integration script for PostHog or similar analytics SDK, including a wrapper function for event tracking.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the analytics SDK in the main application provider and export a trackEvent helper function.'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Journeys
steps.description: Place tracking calls inside your primary interaction handlers to capture user intent and drop-off points.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Help me identify the specific files in my project where I should insert trackEvent calls to measure button clicks and form submissions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Instrument the login and sign-up handlers with analytics tracking calls using the helper function.'

steps.title: Validate Event Flow
steps.description: Ensure your events are actually reaching the analytics dashboard before you consider the feature 'live'.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a testing checklist to verify that my analytics events are firing correctly in a local development environment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test script that triggers all analytics events and verifies the payload structure.'

steps.title: Create Your First Dashboard
steps.description: Visualize your data. A simple funnel chart showing how many users land on your site vs. how many actually use your tool is your most powerful asset.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain how to structure a basic funnel chart in my analytics dashboard to visualize the conversion from landing page to core feature usage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Summarize the data collected so far and suggest how to create a conversion funnel visualization.'

pro_tips: Always track user IDs anonymously to comply with privacy standards while maintaining cohort analysis capabilities.
Avoid 'tracking everything.' Focus on 3-5 high-value events to keep your dashboard clean and your data meaningful.
Use feature flags alongside analytics to A/B test UI changes without redeploying your entire application.
Set up automated alerts for significant drops in conversion rate; don't wait for manual reviews to find bugs.
Document your event naming convention (e.g., 'user_signed_up' vs 'signup_clicked') early to prevent technical debt.
content.next_steps: Once your dashboard is live, commit to a 'weekly review' cadence. Look at your data every Sunday, identify one drop-off point, and use Claude Code to ship an improvement by Wednesday.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical visibility to turn your student project into a professional SaaS product. Don't let your code sit in a vacuum; start measuring your impact today. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
